description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the .Go Service and .Sentry template includes to build, lint, containerize, and deploy a Go service named worker to a staging environment with Sentry release tracking. Define stages Prepare, Image, Release, Staging, and Finalise Staging and set the variable DEPLOYMENT_NAME=worker. In the Prepare stage add a Go Build job (extends .Go Service - Binary Build) that runs `go build ./...` to produce the service binary, and a Go Lint job (extends .Go Service - Go Lint) that runs `golint ./...` (or `go vet`) to lint the code. In the Image stage add a Docker Build job (extends .Go Service - Docker Build) that runs `docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA .` to build the container image. In the Release stage add a Docker Release job (extends .Go Service - Docker Release) that runs `docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA` to push the image, and a Sentry Release job (extends .Sentry - Sentry Release) that runs `sentry-cli releases new $CI_COMMIT_SHA` and `sentry-cli releases set-commits --auto $CI_COMMIT_SHA` to create a Sentry release. In the Staging stage add a Deploy job (extends .Go Service - Deploy Staging) that runs a deployment command such as `helm upgrade --install $DEPLOYMENT_NAME ./helm --set image.tag=$CI_COMMIT_SHA` to deploy the image to the staging cluster. In the Finalise Staging stage add a Sentry Deploy job (extends .Sentry - Sentry Deploy) with variable SENTRY_ENV=staging that runs `sentry-cli releases deploys $CI_COMMIT_SHA new -e staging` to notify Sentry of the deployment. Include the external template files /go-service.yml and /sentry.yml from the Cacophony/gitlab-ci project on the master ref. No explicit caches or artifacts are defined, and jobs inherit their triggers from the extended templates.
